page: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-176-standardlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-intensive server instance. It features 176 vCPUs mapped to 88 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 1408.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is highly dense, featuring 38,652 GB of capacity across 12 local NVMe SSDs. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores ranking in the top 10%. The memory subsystem delivers top-tier read-only small block bandwidth of 11,917.19 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth of 445.54 GB/sec. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the middle 50% average. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), contribute to top-tier performance in ray tracing, encryption, and physics simulations, alongside strong performance in LLM prompt processing.

Given its massive memory capacity, high-density local NVMe storage, and strong multi-threaded capabilities, this server offers high resource density for enterprise-scale workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale relational and non-relational databases, in-memory data stores like Redis, high-throughput static web serving, and heavy multi-threaded data processing tasks. The lack of a GPU makes it less suitable for GPU-accelerated deep learning training, though its AMX instructions support efficient CPU-based LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-176-standardlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-intensive server designed for high-density enterprise workloads. It features 176 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 1408.0 GB of RAM and 38,652 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While single-core performance sits in the average tier, the server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This hardware profile makes the instance highly efficient for memory-heavy and storage-intensive applications without requiring GPU acceleration. It is best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-176-standardlssd is a memory-optimized server featuring 176 vCPUs, 1408 GB RAM, and 38,652 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-176-standardlssd features 176 vCPUs, 1408 GB RAM, and 38,652 GB of local NVMe storage for enterprise databases.
tagline: A high-density memory and storage server featuring 176 vCPUs, 1.4 TB RAM, and 38 TB of local NVMe SSD capacity.
bullet_points: 176 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors at 2.7 GHz
1408 GB of RAM providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio
38,652 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 12 drives
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis, and multi-threaded data processing
